    Home › Manufacturers › Tupolev

    Tupolev

    Russia · Founded 1922

    Bombers, airliners, and the first supersonic transport ever to fly.

    Aircraft here3
    Longest65.70 m
    Combined built88
    First flights1957–1981
    The company

    The story so far

    Tupolev has always blurred the line between bomber and airliner — its Tu-114, still the fastest propeller-driven airliner ever built, was a strategic bomber with a passenger fuselage grafted on, and it flew at 770 km/h on contra-rotating propellers.

    The bureau got the Tu-144 into the air two months before Concorde, making it the first supersonic transport ever to fly. Its Tu-160 is the largest and heaviest combat aircraft ever built, and remains in production in modernised form.

    The line-up

    Tupolev aircraft

    Silhouettes are sized by real wingspan — bigger aircraft draw bigger.

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
